statement.en: Second Class Particle Behaviour in Blocking ASEP
statement.summary: We consider any fixed $d\in\mathbb{Z}_{>0}$ number of second class particles in the asymmetric simple exclusion process (ASEP), constructed via a basic coupling of two ASEPs. We give the joint distribution of the positions of the second class particles and also the probability of there being a second class particle at a given site, under the natural blocking measure for ASEP. In order to find these distributions we use results about the number of particles in half-infinite and finite site ranges of ASEP. Our investigations also lead to probabilistic proofs of well-known combinatorial identities; the Durfee rectangles identity, Euler's identity, and the $q$-Binomial Theorem.

proposer: Daniel Adams, Márton Balázs, Jessica Jay

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents rigorous mathematical theorems about ASEP second-class particle distributions with explicit proofs and derivations of classical combinatorial identities. The results are formalized within probability theory and combinatorics, establishing verifiable propositions about blocking measures and particle positions.
